name: Leads Source Attribution — December 2025
subject: Monthly lead-attribution tracker for December 2025. Captures the 18-channel source universe Comeketo tracks leads across (Wedding Wire, The Knot, FB/IG, website, Google, Bark, Fash, Eventective, House Deal, Zola, weddingplanner, Social Organic, Inbound, Referral, Venue Referral, Lead from Rodrigo, Old Lead/Repeat Client, EZ Cater) and 12 named December deals with their attributed source. Header claims 18 total deals closed but tracker body shows 12 — 6 unaccounted, likely incomplete entry.
source_files: Leads Source - FG , IG , Google leads tracker  - Dec 2025.csv

channel_universe.note: Bundled — Facebook, Instagram, and Crisp Chat (on-site web chat widget) treated as one channel.
Bundle with 'I do Society' partner — clarify whether this is paid Google/TikTok ads routed through the I Do Society platform, or three separate channels misbundled.
Likely 'Fash' = Fash.com / Fashr service marketplace. Confirm.
Unclear — possibly 'HouseDeal' venue partner or promotional program. Confirm.
Comma between weddingplanner and partner — possibly 'weddingplanner.com partner' or 'wedding planner (partner)'. Ambiguous.
Leads that come in but aren't source-tagged — catchall bucket.
Distinct from generic Referral — venue partners specifically. Cross-reference with venue_partners.json.
Leads Rodrigo personally brings in. Distinct channel — shows the founder is treated as a lead source.
Corporate catering marketplace — distinct from wedding-vertical platforms.

channel_mix_december_2025.gap_interpretation: Header top-left cell reads 'Total Deals Closed 18' but the named-lead rows only total 12. Either 6 deals weren't entered by name, the total is pulling from a different sheet, or a second attribution category exists. Flag for Ops.
channel_mix_december_2025.reading: Social + owned digital (FB/IG + Website + Social Organic) account for 8 of 12 = 66.7%. Venue referrals are 2/12 = 16.7%. Paid listing platforms (Wedding Wire, The Knot, Bark, etc.) produced only 1 closed deal in December — worth re-examining spend there.

signals_for_broader_strategy.organic_digital_is_dominant: The combined FB/IG/Website/Social Organic produced 2/3 of attributed closes. This is the strongest-performing engine in December and warrants protection.
signals_for_broader_strategy.listing_platform_underperformance: 11 of the 18 channels are listing platforms (Wedding Wire, The Knot, Bark, Fash, Eventective, House Deal, Zola, weddingplanner, Google/TikTok, EZ Cater). Of those, only Wedding Wire hit in December (1 deal). Either the month was quiet for listings or many of these channels aren't pulling weight and should be reviewed for spend/time.
signals_for_broader_strategy.venue_referral_validates_venue_strategy: 2 venue referrals closed — worth flagging against venue_partners.json to see which venues are sending business. This justifies continued investment in venue partnerships.
signals_for_broader_strategy.rodrigo_as_channel: 'Lead from Rodrigo' is literally a dedicated column. Zero in December but non-zero through the year (implied by the column's existence). Rodrigo as a named lead source is worth its own category.
signals_for_broader_strategy.brazilian_church_channel_missing: The 32-church outreach project has no column here. If Andre's work starts producing closed deals, this tracker needs a new channel — 'Brazilian Church Outreach' or 'Faith Community Referral'. Flag for update.

people_refs_in_data: Leads listed here (Victor, Isadora, Jennifer, Joni, etc.) could become people/* records once they become active projects or long-term relationships. Single-month closed deals alone don't warrant a record — wait until they recur or have material next-steps.
cross_references.venue_partners_cross_check: 2 Venue Referral closes — look up which venues via Close CRM. Likely Monoosnock, Harding Allen, Versailles, or ASA Waters based on the top-volume venues in venue_partners.json.
cross_references.source_vs_owner: This tracker only captures source — not which salesperson closed it. Cross-referencing with Close CRM owner field (and sales_team_scorecard_oct_2025.json) would give source × owner mix, which is a richer coaching signal.

feeds_into: knowledge/sales_team_scorecard_oct_2025.json — closed-deal source attribution per person is a scorecard KPI that isn't currently captured.
knowledge/venue_partners.json — venue-referral closes connect venues to real revenue.
projects/brazilian_church_outreach.json — if/when church outreach starts producing closes, this tracker needs a new channel column.
A future channel-performance dashboard — this file is the first ingredient.
gap_to_close: Only 1 month of data. Need a roll-up across 2025-2026 to see channel-mix trends.
No spend/cost data — can't compute channel ROI without it. Paid-listing channels should have monthly spend logged to make the 'are they worth it?' decision clean.

notes: 'Sydneey' is almost certainly a typo for Sydney — flag for source-sheet cleanup.
'Crisp Chat' is a web-chat widget product; its bundling with FB/IG is operationally reasonable but blurs where the lead actually came from.
This file is tiny (single month of one tracker) but the channel taxonomy is the reusable asset — 18 channels is what the whole acquisition engine attributes against.
The first column ('Total Deals Closed 18') functions as both header and KPI — awkward spreadsheet shape. Cleaner would be a separate summary row/tab.

sources.diff: Indexed Dec 2025 lead-source attribution tracker — 18-channel universe captured, 12 named closes attributed (organic digital 66.7%, venue referral 16.7%, listing platforms 8.3%). Header-vs-body gap flagged (18 claimed, 12 named). Brazilian church channel flagged as missing from taxonomy.
